title: Threat Modeling, Privacy Engineering, and Abuse-Resistant Design · Product Forensics
summary: A preserved decision record for threat modeling, privacy engineering, and abuse-resistant design contains one plausible evidence, scope, design, implementation, evaluation, or ownership defect. Find the first unsupported claim before changing the product. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in threat, privacy, and abuse assurance case and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal…
objectives: Trace assets, actors, entry points, data flows, trust boundaries, attack paths, controls, detection, response, and residual risk.
Prove deny-by-default resource and action authorization across interface, service, data, cache, job, event, export, and support paths.
Analyze data action, affected people, likelihood, problematic consequence, controls, tradeoffs, and governance across the lifecycle.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# capstone-project cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: capstone-project
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug Threat Model System
steps.instruction:
debug case — Authentication, encryption, a scanner, and a policy page substitute for a system-specific threat model, privacy risk assessment, abuse case, and response plan. The debug variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Trace assets, actors, entry points, data flows, trust boundaries, attack paths, controls, detection, response, and residual risk.

Inspect the Threat Model System trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Inspect the failure record · Debug Threat Model System.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for trace assets, actors, entry points, data flows, trust boundaries, attack paths, controls, detection, response, and residual risk. For Inspect the failure record · Debug Threat Model System, keep Threat Model System connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: For Threat Model System, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: For Inspect the failure record · Debug Threat Model System, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Threat Model System precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Inspect the failure record · Debug Threat Model System, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: Authentication, encryption, a scanner, and a policy page substitute for a system-specific threat model, privacy risk assessment, abuse case, and response plan. The debug variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Trace assets, actors, entry points, data flows, trust boundaries, attack paths, controls, detection, response, and residual risk.

For Inspect the failure record · Debug Threat Model System, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A generic threat checklist is a project threat model.

stimulus.title: Threat Model System case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Authentication, encryption, a scanner, and a policy page substitute for a system-specific threat model, privacy risk assessment, abuse case, and response plan. The debug variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Trace assets, actors, entry points, data flows, trust boundaries, attack paths, controls, detection, response, and residual risk.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: A generic threat checklist is a project threat model.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Trace assets, actors, entry points, data flows, trust boundaries, attack paths, controls, detection, response, and residual risk. Artifact under review: threat, privacy, and abuse assurance case.

options.text: The Threat Model System record says threat, privacy, and abuse assurance case looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the inspect the failure record evidence boundary for Threat Model System.

For inspect the failure record, apply Threat Model System without its stated preconditions, omit the threat, privacy, and abuse assurance case trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Threat Model System for threat, privacy, and abuse assurance case.
The record names the assumption, applies Threat Model System, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

content.code:
capstone-vulnerability-response-yi9hsr:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Vulnerability Response structural check is only one gate. threat, privacy, and abuse assurance case must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

steps.title: Publish the incident record · Debug Vulnerability Response
steps.instruction:
Write the debug evidence note for Vulnerability Response: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Authentication, encryption, a scanner, and a policy page substitute for a system-specific threat model, privacy risk assessment, abuse case, and response plan. The debug variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Define reporting, triage, severity, containment, remediation, disclosure, dependency response, verification, and learning ownership.

For Publish the incident record · Debug Vulnerability Response, keep Vulnerability Response connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Define reporting, triage, severity, containment, remediation, disclosure, dependency response, verification, and learning ownership.
